signup.title: Create your account
signup.subtitle: Pick a username, then a display name
signup.usernameHelp: 3–20 characters. English letters, digits, underscore. Used to sign in.
signup.nicknameHelp: Shown to other players. Persian or Latin letters, digits, spaces.
signup.passwordHelp: At least 4 characters. You'll need it to sign in.

error.invalidUsername: Username must be 3–20 characters: English letters, digits, underscore, starting with a letter.
error.invalidNickname: Display name must be 2–20 characters (letters, digits, spaces).
error.shortPassword: Password must be at least 4 characters.
error.serverError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.tagline: Play Persian card games with friends — online and free.

hokm.name: Hokm
hokm.desc: The classic Persian trick-taking game. Choose trump, follow suit, race to 7 points.

shelem.name: Shelem
shelem.desc: The Persian partnership bidding game. Win the auction, set trump with your first lead, and capture Aces, 10s, 5s and tricks to make your contract.

pasur.name: Pasur
pasur.desc: The classic fishing game (Chahar Barg). Capture cards that sum to 11, sweep the pool, win the clubs.

features.title: Why Varagh?
features.f1: Sign up with just a username — no email or phone
features.f2: Works on any device, any screen size
features.f3: Private rooms — share a 6-letter code with friends

s1.title: Create an account
s1.desc: Just a username and password — no email needed

s3.title: Play!
s3.desc: First team to 7 points wins the game

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.subtitle: Questions, feedback, or want to say hi? Reach out.

install.title: Install Varagh
install.subtitle: Add Varagh to your home screen for a faster, full-screen, app-like experience.
install.installed: Varagh is already installed on this device. 🎉

ios.title: On iPhone & iPad (Safari)
ios.s1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of the screen.
ios.s2: Scroll down and tap “Add to Home Screen”.
ios.s3: Tap “Add” — Varagh now lives on your home screen.

android.title: On Android (Chrome)
android.s1: Open the browser menu (⋮) in the top corner.
android.s2: Tap “Install app” or “Add to Home screen”.
android.s3: Confirm — Varagh installs like a native app.

desktop.title: On desktop (Chrome / Edge)
desktop.s1: Click the install icon in the address bar.
desktop.s2: Or open the browser menu and choose “Install Varagh”.
desktop.s3: Confirm to add it to your apps.

learn.title: Learn the games
learn.subtitle: New to Persian card games? Here's a quick beginner's guide to each one.

hokm.name: Hokm
hokm.goal: Win rounds by taking the most tricks. The first side to reach 7 points wins the game.
basics: Each round one player is the Hakem (ruler). After seeing their first 5 cards, they pick the trump suit (hokm).
Players take turns playing one card. You must follow the led suit if you can; otherwise you may play any card.
A trick is won by the highest trump played — or, if no trump was played, the highest card of the led suit.
Taking 7 tricks wins the round for 1 point. Sweeping all 7 (a 'kot') is worth extra points.
In 4-player games you play in teams of two sitting across; in 2 and 3 player games it's every player for themselves.
tips: Save trump cards — they beat everything and are perfect for stealing tricks.
Lead with a long suit to make opponents follow and burn through their cards.
Remember which high cards are already gone, so you know when your card is safe to win.
As Hakem, choose the suit you hold the most of as trump.

pasur.name: Pasur
pasur.goal: Capture valuable cards from the pool. The first player to reach the target score (62 by default) wins.
basics: Four cards lie face-up in the central pool. On your turn you play one card from your hand.
A number card captures pool cards that add up to exactly 11 with it (for example, a 4 captures a 7).
A Jack scoops up every number card in the pool at once; Queens and Kings capture only by matching rank.
Clearing the whole pool with a capture is a 'Sur' and scores 5 bonus points.
Points come from Aces, Jacks, the 2♣ and 10♦, plus a big bonus for holding the most clubs at the end of the round.
tips: Hold a Jack until the pool is full, so it captures the most cards at once.
Chase clubs — having the most clubs is worth a flat 7 points.
The 10♦ (3 points) and 2♣ (2 points) are prize cards; grab them whenever you can.
Watch for Sur chances to clear the pool and steal 5 quick points.

shelem.name: Shelem
shelem.goal: With your partner across the table, win the auction and capture enough card-points (Aces, 10s, 5s) and tricks to make your bid. The first team to the target score wins.
basics: Four players in two teams sit across — you and the player opposite are partners.
Each player is dealt 12 cards; the last 4 form the Zamin (زمین), face-down in the middle.
Bidding rises in steps of 5 with no minimum, starting left of the dealer. Passing is permanent, and a Shelem (slam) call beats every number.
The highest bidder becomes the Hakem (declarer): they take the 4 Zamin cards, then bury any 4 face-down — those buried cards count for the Hakem's team.
There is no separate trump pick: the Hakem leads any card to the first trick, and that card's suit becomes trump.
Follow the led suit if you can; otherwise play anything. The highest trump wins a trick, else the highest card of the led suit.
Card-points: each Ace = 10, each 10 = 10, each 5 = 5, and each trick won = 5 — 165 points in a round.
The Hakem's team must reach its bid to score it; the opponents always score exactly what they captured. First team to the target score (default 1165) wins.
tips: Only bid what your long suit and high cards can truly take — a failed contract is subtracted from your score.
As Hakem, bury your weakest off-suit cards in the Zamin to create voids you can trump later.
Feed Aces, 10s and 5s into tricks your partner already controls; throw your worthless cards when an opponent has the trick.
Failed-contract penalty (table option): ‘simple’ loses the bid; ‘doubled’ doubles it when your team trails the opponents; ‘yasa’ costs the full 165 when your team trails.
Zamin reveal (option): ‘private’ shows the Zamin only to the Hakem; ‘reveal’ turns it face-up to everyone before the Hakem takes it.
More options: Shelem reward (a flat 330 or double the bid), Ace value (10 → 165 per round, or 15 → 185), and Successful score (exactly the bid, or the actual points made).
